title: Session Layer
description: Establishes, manages, and terminates sessions between applications.
fullExplanation: The Session Layer manages dialog control between applications. It establishes, maintains, and terminates communication sessions. A session is a logical connection that allows data exchange. This layer handles session checkpointing (saving state), recovery (resuming after failure), and synchronization. It also manages full-duplex (both directions simultaneously) or half-duplex (one direction at a time) communication.
keyFacts: Synchronization points allow resuming after interruption
Often combined with Application layer in TCP/IP model
Examples: NetBIOS, PPTP, RPC, SQL sessions

functions.name: Session Establishment
functions.description: Set up logical connection between applications, negotiate parameters

functions.name: Session Maintenance
functions.description: Keep session alive, handle timeouts, manage state

functions.name: Dialog Control
functions.description: Manage who can transmit (full-duplex or half-duplex)

functions.name: Synchronization
functions.description: Insert checkpoints to allow recovery from failures

protocols.name: NetBIOS
protocols.description: Network Basic Input/Output System - session services for Windows networks

protocols.name: RPC
protocols.description: Remote Procedure Call - execute code on remote systems

protocols.name: SMB
